rationale: Both correctly diagnose the config/tokenizer mismatch, cite the same crash line, and match the thread's workaround and 'open a new issue' disposition. X stays tighter to the evidence and adds a concrete, clearly-labeled remediation code snippet plus a hedged observation about doc headers. Y asserts unsupported specifics as fact — 'confirmed this works on NPU hardware' (the collect_env output shows a CUDA-less aarch64 CPU box, no NPU mentioned anywhere) and attributes the `_ratio_other_slices` code to PR #2713 when the thread only credits that PR with the config-topology change — plus an unverified config table (i2t/t2t rows) not grounded in the given thread.
